methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $2.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $8.6B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Wayve
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $8.6B

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $7B

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Hive AI is a Series D AI & Software company founded by Kevin Guo, currently valued at $2.0B post-money following $120M+ in total fundraising. The valuation assessment draws from a comp universe of 9 private peers (Wayve, Cohere, Hugging Face, ElevenLabs, Labelbox, and others) and 1 public comparable (PLTR).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement, yields $3.0B from the current $2.0B post-money base. Peer-median analysis across 5 same-stage, same-sector comparables produces $4.5B, derived from valuations ranging from $1.0B (Labelbox) to $8.6B (Wayve), with median peer valuation of $4.5B.

The triangulated analysis implies a fair-value range of $2.5B to $5.2B, with a median of $3.8B. This suggests the current $2.0B valuation sits in the lower quartile of the peer distribution, consistent with stage and capital raise profile. Key uncertainties include timing to next funding event, revenue traction relative to peer set, and market conditions affecting late-stage AI venture multiples. The wide peer range ($1.0B–$8.6B) reflects significant dispersion in Series D valuations within the sector, signaling that company-specific execution metrics will materially influence valuation trajectory.
